relations.note: Scaling note refines the default geometry values set in the visual composition rule.
Fallback guidance addresses client limitations for the absolute-positioned badge defined in the composition rule.
Radius lock and shadow rule are both mandatory, never-change specs layered onto the same composition.
Shadow requirement co-applies with the base composition.

quotes: Light Blue `#E6F0F9` fill, single accent shape.
the **left padding must clear the overhanging badge half plus a gap** (? `W/2 + 24px`)
Brand Blue `#00529b`, Nunito Sans weight 800, ~21px / 28px line-height, left-aligned, **vertically centered** to the box
a **White `#ffffff` rounded square** holding the line icon (Brand Blue stroke)
Let `W` = badge width (use **80px**, icon ?48px inside).
Inset the blue box from the left by **`W/2` = 40px**
A **light-blue accent-shape box** containing a short blue headline, with a **white icon badge that straddles the box's left edge**
Used for supporting "proof point" statements (dosing convenience, time-to-effect, etc.).
Never let copy slide under the badge.

rules.intent: Lock the exact half-on/half-off badge composition and fills so the icon callout renders identically across instances.
rules.rule_text: The Icon Callout Box is a Light Blue accent-shape box (icon_callout.box.fill = #E6F0F9) sitting on the page/section background, holding left-aligned, vertically-centered Brand Blue (#00529b) ExtraBold text (~21px/28px line-height, never sliding under the badge). Its left padding must clear the overhanging badge half plus a 24px gap. A White (#ffffff) rounded-square icon badge (80px, 48px inner icon, Brand Blue stroke) straddles the box's left edge, inset by half the badge width (W/2 = 40px) so its center lands exactly on the box's left edge (0px offset), vertically centered via a -40px margin-top. Used for supporting proof-point statements such as dosing convenience or time-to-effect.
rules.snippets: <div style="position:relative; padding-left:40px;"> ... <td style="background-color:#E6F0F9; border-radius:40px 8px 40px 8px; padding:26px 34px 26px 64px;"> ... <div style="position:absolute; left:0; top:50%; margin-top:-40px; width:80px; height:80px; background-color:#ffffff; border-radius:20px 4px 20px 4px; box-shadow:0 4px 10px rgba(1,30,69,0.18);">

quotes: <div style="position:relative; padding-left:40px;">
background-color:#E6F0F9; border-radius:40px 8px 40px 8px; padding:26px 34px 26px 64px;
box `border-radius: 40px 8px 40px 8px;`
`border-radius: 20px 4px 20px 4px;`
Corner radius (mandatory, never change): box `border-radius: 40px 8px 40px 8px;`
The white badge **mirrors the same accent shape at smaller scale**, `border-radius: 20px 4px 20px 4px;`
Never simplify either to a single uniform radius, swap the corners, or flip the diagonal.

rules.intent: Preserve the signature asymmetric accent-shape geometry as a locked, never-varying brand device.
rules.rule_text: The box radius is fixed at 40px 8px 40px 8px (TL TR BR BL, 5:1 ratio, large on top-left/bottom-right, near-point on top-right/bottom-left) and the badge mirrors the same accent shape at smaller scale, fixed at 20px 4px 20px 4px (also 5:1). Never simplify either to a single uniform radius, swap the corners, or flip the diagonal.

quotes: `box-shadow: 0 4px 10px rgba(1, 30, 69, 0.18);`
the white icon badge **must carry a subtle drop shadow** so it lifts off the blue box
This shadow is required on every instance; without it the badge looks flat and pasted-on.

rules.intent: Ensure the badge reads as a layered, floating element rather than a flat cutout.
rules.rule_text: The white icon badge must always carry a subtle drop shadow (Dark Navy at 18% opacity, e.g. 0 4px 10px rgba(1,30,69,0.18)) on every instance so it visually lifts off the blue box; omitting it makes the badge look flat and pasted-on.

quotes: Outlook (desktop, Word engine) ignores
pre-composite the white badge (with its shadow, accent radii, and icon) as a transparent PNG
ship the live version above and accept the Outlook degrade

rules.intent: Manage known Outlook rendering limitations without compromising fidelity on capable clients.
rules.rule_text: The 50/50 badge overhang relies on position:absolute, which Outlook desktop (Word engine) ignores, dropping the badge to the top-left in normal flow with no box inset. For pixel-perfect fidelity across all clients, pre-composite the white badge (with its shadow, accent radii, and icon) as a transparent PNG placed via the same absolute positioning, or bake the badge-on-edge treatment into the box's background image while keeping text live. When that isn't feasible, ship the live MJML version and accept the Outlook degrade (badge flush at the inner-left edge); every other spec (Light Blue fill, 40px 8px 40px 8px radius, blue 800 text, drop shadow) stays identical.

quotes: Defaults: badge size `W = 80px`, inner icon `48px`.
**inset = `W/2`**, **box left-padding = `W/2 + 24px`**, **badge `margin-top` = `?H/2`**
Defaults: badge size `W = 80px`, inner icon `48px`.
If your real badge is a different size, keep the relationships

rules.intent: Guarantee the badge-on-edge geometry remains mathematically exact at any badge scale.
rules.rule_text: Default badge width is 80px with a 48px inner icon. If a real badge uses a different size, the proportional relationships must be preserved: inset = W/2, box left-padding = W/2 + 24px, badge margin-top = -H/2, so the perpendicular-bisector edge split stays exact.
